summary: Lo, Patel, and Yıldız proved an approximate version of Jackson's conjecture in 2022 (every $d$-regular oriented graph on $n > n_0$ vertices with $d \geq (1/4+\varepsilon)n$ has a Hamilton cycle). Their 2023 follow-up establishes Jackson's conjecture itself for sufficiently large $n$: there exists $n_0$ such that every $d$-regular oriented graph on $n \geq n_0$ vertices with $n \leq 4d+1$ has a Hamilton cycle. The original conjecture for all $d > 2$ (including small values) remains open.

since_posted.title: Hamilton Cycles in Dense Regular Digraphs and Oriented Graphs
since_posted.authors: Allan Lo, Viresh Patel, Mehmet Akif Yıldız
since_posted.venue: Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B
since_posted.claim: Proves that for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $n_0$ such that every $d$-regular oriented graph on $n > n_0$ vertices with $d \geq (1/4 + \varepsilon)n$ has a Hamilton cycle, giving an approximate version of Jackson's conjecture.

since_posted.title: Cycle Partitions in Dense Regular Digraphs and Oriented Graphs
since_posted.authors: Allan Lo, Viresh Patel, Mehmet Akif Yıldız
since_posted.claim: Proves Jackson's conjecture for sufficiently large $n$: there exists $n_0$ such that every $d$-regular oriented graph on $n \geq n_0$ vertices with $n \leq 4d+1$ has a Hamilton cycle, via a cycle-partition result showing at most $n/(2d+1)$ vertex-disjoint cycles suffice.

search_queries: Jackson conjecture Hamilton cycle d-diregular oriented graph 4d+1 vertices
Hamilton cycles d-regular oriented graphs small order Kühn Osthus
diregular oriented graph Hamilton cycle conjecture progress 2013 2024 2025
arXiv "d-diregular" OR "diregular oriented" Hamilton cycle conjecture Jackson
notes: arXiv:1907.08479 (Liebenau–Pehova, 2019) addresses a different Jackson conjecture about bipartite tournaments and is excluded from since_posted. The ScienceDirect page for 2203.10112 (JCTB) returned HTTP 403; DOI not confirmed. The 2309.11677 result proves Jackson's conjecture for all sufficiently large n (leaving finitely many small-d cases open); the exact value of n₀ is not specified explicitly. The Kühn–Osthus variant (strongly 2-connected, at most 6d vertices) appears to remain open.
